Any seed being soaked a hunderd times in a paste of Ahkola
fruit or in its oil, or in a paste or oil of 6lesmataka {Sebesten) fruit,
will, when sown in a soil mixed with hall, sprout instantaneously,
and what wonder would it he, if its branches were laden with
fruits 1

A wise man should remove the shell of the Sleimataka (Sebes-
ten) seeds, soak them in water mixed with the slimy paste of
Ahkola (Alangium) fruits and dry them in shade seven times. Then
rub them with buffaloes dung, and lay them in their dry dung.

Treatment of Trees LV 535
Being sown thereafter in a soil soaked with hail-water, they will
bear fruit in a day.
